summary: Iter 8 — P1 Brain Activation backend tests. All 13 backend tests PASS. /api/orchestrator/health confirmed with all 6 subscribers wired (job_rejected, interview_completed, offer_received, job_applied, recruiter_reachout, bookmark_added — each count=1). Emergent LLM provider OK and any_available=true. /api/orchestrator/telemetry, /memory, /recent-events properly require auth (401). All 5 /api/decision/* routes mount correctly and enforce auth via Depends(get_current_user). Existing routes (/api/applications POST+PATCH, /api/bookmarks/{job_id}, /api/emails/{id}/classify) still mount and enforce auth (no route regression after event publish wiring). Backend startup logs are clean — no exceptions from event_bus or wire_subscribers; all 6 subscribers register successfully on each reload. Telemetry endpoint shape verified by code review matches the requested schema: window_days, total_ai_calls, success_rate, latency_ms{p50,p95,max}, by_feature, by_task, event_throughput, bus_global.

telemetry_shape_code_review.empty_dataset_handling: returns zeros for total_ai_calls, success_rate=0.0, latencies={p50:0,p95:0,max:0} — verified by reading routes_orchestrator.py

critical_code_review_comments: orchestrator.py wire_subscribers() correctly registers all 6 subscribers. Subscribers write to dedicated Mongo collections (workflow_hints, salary_comparison, interview_prep_context, cv_tailor_hints) and call CareerIntelligence.record_event(). Idempotency relies on event_bus.subscribe() being deduplicated — verify event_bus.subscribe handles duplicate registration safely (subscribers count should stay at 1 on repeated startup reloads, which logs confirm).

on_job_applied handler is effectively a no-op (the conditional `if False else None` short-circuits the insert because career_events is already written by bus.publish). The comment explains this is intentional; consider removing the dead code for clarity in a future cleanup.

routes_decision.py — decision_match references `decisions` collection via mongo_db.decisions but no index is created on (user_id, job_id) for the upsert. Add an index to keep this performant as it scales.

routes_orchestrator.py telemetry uses ISO-string comparison for created_at ($gte: since). This works because timestamps are stored as ISO strings throughout the codebase, but it's fragile if any insertion path stores BSON Date. Confirmed all writes in orchestrator.py / event_bus / subscribers use .isoformat() → consistent.

routes_decision.py strategic_career_plan and skill_gap_analysis call ci.cross_feature_signals() but don't catch its failure. If that helper throws, the endpoint 500s before reaching orchestrator.run. Consider try/except around the signal fetch for graceful degradation.
Telemetry endpoint exposes per-user data only (filtered by uid). Good privacy posture. bus_global is shared/global which is acceptable for diagnostic purposes.
test_credentials: No static creds — Emergent OAuth. Auth-gated endpoints verified via 401 path only, per request directive.
context_for_next_testing_agent: P1 Brain Activation backend pieces are verified: 6 event subscribers wired, telemetry endpoint live, all 5 migrated /api/decision/* routes mount and require auth, event-publishing routes (applications/bookmarks/email-classify) unchanged in mount/auth behavior. Tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iter8_p1_brain.py are non-destructive and can be re-run anytime. Authenticated end-to-end telemetry data validation (with real LLM calls populating ai_telemetry) was NOT exercised here per request — main agent already manually verified that path. Frontend was not retested this iteration (backend-only request).
